market_overview.headline: Markets push higher despite geopolitical tensions, with 40% of stocks in Full Bull mode but both SPY and QQQ showing tight EMA spreads that could reverse.
market_overview.detail: Strong breadth with 507 of 800 stocks in bullish signals (63%), led by Electronic Technology and Energy sectors. However, SPY and QQQ both showing minimal EMA separation with bearish cross risk warnings.

reversal_candidates.headline: Bear Rally Reversals — EMA Crossovers Pending

items.name: Meta Platforms, Inc.
items.note: Mega-cap tech name rallying hard off bear structure with momentum building for bullish EMA cross.

items.name: UnitedHealth Group Incorporated
items.note: Healthcare leader showing strong reversal momentum with imminent bullish crossover setup.

items.name: JP Morgan Chase & Co.
items.crossover_detail: 8W just below 13W (0.29%) — bullish cross potential; 13W just below 21W (0.27%) — bullish cross potential
items.note: Banking giant with dual crossover potential — both 8W/13W and 13W/21W setups aligning.

items.name: Bank of America Corporation
items.crossover_detail: 8W just below 13W (0.33%) — bullish cross potential; 13W just below 21W (0.32%) — bullish cross potential
items.note: Major bank showing similar dual crossover setup as JPM — sector rotation into financials brewing.

items.name: Berkshire Hathaway Inc. New
items.crossover_detail: 8W just below 13W (0.74%) — bullish cross potential; 13W just below 21W (0.61%) — bullish cross potential
items.note: Buffett's conglomerate showing rare dual crossover potential from deep bear structure.

items.name: Valero Energy Corporation
items.note: Energy refiner pulling back on high volume — watch for potential institutional distribution before entry.

items.name: Baker Hughes Company
items.note: Oil services leader with elevated volume on pullback — proceed with caution on entry timing.

items.name: L3Harris Technologies, Inc.
items.note: Defense contractor showing orderly pullback on normal volume — healthier setup for entry.

items.name: Williams Companies, Inc. (The)
items.note: Pipeline operator with controlled selling volume suggests institutional accumulation opportunity.

momentum_themes.headline: Electronic Tech Leading Momentum Surge
momentum_themes.detail: Electronic Technology stocks dominating momentum with massive spread scores, led by memory/storage names. Semiconductor equipment and optical networking also accelerating hard.

sector_analysis.headline: Energy vs Tech Bifurcation

strongest.note: Perfect bullish score but mostly in pullback mode — rotation out of energy accelerating.
Industrial strength continuing with 16 of 18 names in Full Bull mode.
Tech momentum building with 32 Full Bull names leading semiconductor surge.

weakest.note: Software stocks remain under pressure despite some META/MSFT bear rally attempts.
Auto and housing-sensitive names struggling with only 1 of 5 stocks bullish.
Service sector weakness persisting with 5 of 6 names in bearish structures.

sector_analysis.detail: Clear rotation from software/services into hardware/semiconductors and industrials. Energy showing distribution despite bullish structure.

items.text: SPY and QQQ both showing minimal EMA separation with bearish cross risk warnings — broad market vulnerable to reversal.
High volume pullbacks in energy sector (VLO, BKR, FANG) suggest institutional selling pressure.
BTC still in bear structure (-5% vs 21W) creating drag on risk-on sentiment despite recent bounce.
Technology Services sector collapse (-57.6% net score) could spread to hardware if Iran tensions escalate.

news_drivers.headline: What Moved Markets
news_drivers.summary: Iran conflict escalation dominated headlines with energy infrastructure and shipping concerns driving sector rotation. Defense contractors benefited while airlines/shipping faced pressure. Geopolitical risk premium building despite market resilience.

items.text: Iran war escalation driving energy price volatility and supply chain concerns
Strait of Hormuz shipping disruptions supporting energy names despite broader rotation
Defense sector strength on North Korea missile tests and Iran conflict expansion
EU pushing jet fuel diversification plans supporting energy infrastructure plays
Bangladesh fuel price hikes reflect global energy supply tightening from Iran war
